deck: Part three of Dimagi's culture series: four team members unpack the value "Evolve by learning and sharing openly," from out-collaborating rather than out-competing to learning candidly from failure.
seo_description: Four Dimagi leaders unpack the value "Evolve by learning and sharing openly": out-collaborating, starting with why, using data, and speaking up.
og_description: Dimagi's culture series, part 3: out-collaborate, start with why, use data, and speak up. Learning and sharing openly.

pull_quote.text: We always approach a new partner as a unique new project that we're starting from scratch. We have a strong product, we have a strong method, but we're always looking to learn through our partners.

bullets: Out-collaborate, not out-compete: why partnering across the industry beats competing in a field with too little profit to win alone
Start with why: how shared understanding of goals makes feedback easier to give and prevents misaligned priorities
Partners, not vendors: approaching every new partner as a fresh slate and learning through them, even after years across health, agriculture, and education
Learning from failure: no-blame retrospectives, owning your mistakes, and why that is harder at the individual level
Speak up: the discomfort of offering constructive feedback, and why Dimagi trains teams to give it well
Data to drive decisions: the annual engagement survey, the field visit where "average" meant something different on the ground, and acting on what the data shows
A growth mindset: how leaning into new and uncomfortable skills makes Dimagi a place where teams get smarter fast

guests.name: Lucina Tse
guests.role: Chief Operating Officer, reflecting on how the value shows up externally with partners and internally through people operations.

guests.name: Gillian Javetski
guests.role: Chief of Staff, on pairing bold ideas with the discipline to start with why and stay open to feedback.

guests.name: Avni Singhal
guests.role: Senior Customer Success Manager, on out-collaborating with partners and owning up to your own mistakes.

guests.name: Simon Kelly
guests.role: Director of Server Engineering and Dimagi values council member, on team learning, starting with why, and learning from failure.
